title: Extractive spectrophotometric determination of trace amounts of copper(II) using 1,5-diphenyl-1,4-pentadien-3-oxime
author: N. Y. Sreedhar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Sri Venkateswara University, Tirupati-517 502. India 
 E-mail  : sreedhar_ny2001 @yahoo.co.in              Fax:  91-877-2248485 
 Manuscript received 23 August 2004, revised 5 April 2005. accepted 17 August 2005  

 1,5-Dipheny1-1.4-pentadien-3-oxime (DPPDO) has been identified as a sensitive and selective analytical reagent for the extractive spectrophotometric determination of copper(II). This reagent reacts with copper(II) in the pH range of 2.0-12.0 to form a yellowish-red 1 : 1 complex in chloroform with a absorbance maximum at 503 nm having molar absorptivity and Sandell's Sensitivity values of 1.57 x 10 4  mol -1  cm -1  and 0.004065 µg cm -2  respectively. The developed extractive spectrophotometric method has successfully been employed for the determination of Cu II  in leafy vegetables etc.
